acceptance_rules: Every new module must declare audience, slot, owner, status, one plain-language label, one-sentence description, and one primary link before it can appear in NDYRA.
External modules stay off the simple member home until they are intentionally bridged or explicitly approved as boundary shells.
Operator-only tools never appear in the public or member on-ramp.
If a module brings multiple tools, the simple home only gets one primary action. The rest belongs on the More page.
Member-facing copy on first-run surfaces should avoid acronyms and internal jargon whenever possible.
Signals are alerts. Stories are content. Do not blur the two when adding new social surfaces.
Biometric data stays private by default and may only surface through explicit member controls.
For returning members, For You is the familiar default entry. Simple Home stays available as the calmer backup.

slots.description: Large first-run choices for people who are brand new to NDYRA.
Essential next actions only. No overload. No jargon.
Expanded tools once the member explicitly asks for more.
Device-side experience controls and future module settings bridges.
Operator-only module truth and release wiring.
External modules and paused seams that should not silently leak into the simple flow.

public_choices.label: I want to join as a member
public_choices.description: See member pricing and start with the basics.

public_choices.label: I already have an account
public_choices.description: Go straight to sign in and pick up where you left off.

public_choices.label: I run a gym
public_choices.description: Use the separate gym path without mixing it into the member flow.

modules.label: Find my gym
modules.description: Choose the gym NDYRA should center around for classes, community, and challenges.
modules.plain_description: Pick your gym first so NDYRA knows what to show you.

modules.label: Messages and alerts
modules.description: Keep direct messages, requests, announcements, and alerts in one calm place. Signals stay alerts, not story content.
modules.plain_description: Read your messages and alerts in one place. Stories live somewhere else on purpose.

modules.label: My profile and settings
modules.description: Manage your profile, privacy, health-device settings, fitness bio mounts, timezone, and comfort settings from one place.
modules.plain_description: This is where you manage your account, device settings, privacy, and how NDYRA feels on this device.

modules.label: My workouts
modules.description: Profile-level workout refs live here, while the full video workout library stays in the separate Timer module.
modules.plain_description: Keep the workouts you saved. The full video library is still part of the separate Timer module.

modules.label: For You, stories, and recaps
modules.description: Follow people and gyms, browse For You, open Stories, and see shareable workout recaps without crowding the calm backup home.
modules.plain_description: Open the social side of NDYRA when you want the main feed, stories, and recap sharing.

modules.label: Challenges and events
modules.description: Join challenges, track progress, and RSVP to events once the member wants more than the essentials.
modules.plain_description: Use this when you want goals, events, and momentum tools.

modules.label: Performance and biometrics
modules.description: Reserved dashboard, device settings, and fitness bio surfaces for the biometrics module without pretending ingestion is already integrated.
modules.plain_description: This area is ready for fitness data, charts, and a performance dashboard once the biometrics module hands back cleanly.

modules.label: Wallet, shop, and receipts
modules.description: Top up tokens, browse the shop, review purchases, and inspect account entitlements without pushing money surfaces into the first screen.
modules.plain_description: Open this when you need tokens, purchases, or account billing.

modules.label: Timer module boundary
modules.description: The full Timer runtime stays separate. Core only shows the boundary honestly while BRG01 prepares the recap and replay bridge.
modules.plain_description: Timer is a separate module right now. Core only shows the boundary and the future replay bridge honestly.

modules.label: For gyms / business tools
modules.description: Business operations stay in their own workflow instead of leaking into the member on-ramp.
modules.plain_description: Gyms follow a separate path so members do not get overwhelmed.

modules.label: Admin and release tools
modules.description: Operator-only status, wiring, execute, and release closeout truth surfaces.

modules.label: Check-In / attendance spine
modules.description: Paused shared platform/backend spine. Core now keeps both member and business boundary shells honest until the lane is formally resumed.
modules.plain_description: Check-In is still paused, but both the member and business boundary shells are now in place.

notes: Core can hide or disable a module lane at the host level without rewriting the shared shell.
A disabled module must fail locally and never take down unrelated Core routes.
Kill switches are host safety controls, not proof that a module is integrated.
